title: Enforcement Error Resolved by Successful Detection
description: Detects when an app reported an enforcement error (unmapped exit code) but was subsequently detected as installed. The error is informational — the app installed despite the unmapped exit code.
explanation:
An app installation initially reported an **enforcement error** (unmapped exit code), but the IME detection check subsequently found the app **is installed**.

This happens when the MSI/EXE installer returns a non-standard exit code (e.g., 1603) that has no mapping configured in the Intune app definition. The IME treats unmapped exit codes as errors in its enforcement state, but when detection runs afterward, it confirms the app is present on the device.

**The app is installed and functional.** The enforcement error is an Intune app model artifact, not a real installation failure.

remediation.title: Configure exit code mapping in Intune
steps: Open the Intune portal > Apps > [affected app] > Properties > Edit
In the Return codes section, add a mapping for the reported exit code
Map the exit code to 'Success' or 'Soft reboot' depending on the installer behavior
This prevents the enforcement error from appearing in future deployments

relatedDocs.title: Win32 app return codes in Intune
